title: 6 Facts About Jains in India
overview: Jainism is one of the world's oldest religions, originating in India. Jains make up only 0.4% of India's population but stand out in education, wealth, diet, and certain social attitudes.

key_facts.title: Geographic Concentration
key_facts.description: Jains are heavily concentrated in Western India, especially Maharashtra. 4% of Mumbai's population is Jain.
key_facts.details: Few Jains remain in Eastern India despite historical origins there. Migration to the West began around 300 B.C.E.

key_facts.title: Education and Wealth
key_facts.description: Jains are significantly more educated and wealthier than the Indian average.

statistics.caste: Majority belong to General Category (higher caste). Only 20% in lower castes (SC/ST/OBC) vs 68% nationally.

key_facts.title: Dietary Practices
key_facts.description: Strong adherence to ahimsa (non-violence) through vegetarianism.

key_facts.title: Relationship with Hinduism
key_facts.description: Jains perceive strong commonality with Hindus, but the feeling is not strongly reciprocated.

key_facts.title: Social Segregation Preferences
key_facts.description: Jains prefer living separately from other religious and caste groups.

key_facts.title: Political Leaning
key_facts.description: Strong preference for the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

methodology_summary: Based on a national survey of 29,999 Indian adults (including 109 Jains) conducted Nov 2019 – March 2020. Data weighted to 2011 census.
